Spring 2023, the Environmental Systems Science major will accept a one-year sequence of Algebra/Trigonometry-based physics completed at another institution, in addition to calculus-based physics offered at community colleges. Although not a direct course equivalent, the one-year sequence of Algebra/Trigonometry-based physics can be substituted for the major requirements for PHYS 018/018L and PHYS 019/019L. Students who only have one semester of Algebra/Trigonometry-based physics will not meet the prereqs for PHYS 009 and 019 and would have to complete the series at a Community College.</p><p><br></p><p>For students completing the sequence at a California Community College, courses with <a href=\"https://c-id.net/courses\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">C-IDs PHYS 105 and PHYS 110 or PHYS 100 S</a> will be accepted towards the requirement. Students electing to meet the requirement with Algebra/Trigonometry-based physics may be unable to take advanced courses in biophysics and other courses requiring calculus-based physics. Please meet with your Community College Academic Counselor to discuss the Algebra/Trigonometry-based physics course option further.</p>","groupId":"4fd76f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":5,"area":"General"},{"type":"RequirementGroup","hideSectionLetters":true,"sections":[{"type":"Section","rows":[{"position":0,"cells":[{"type":"Requirement","requirement":{"name":"Natural Sciences or Engineering courses"},"requirementAttributes":[],"id":"7909a29e-debb-4a2e-6edf-08dd9e07c4d5","position":0,"attributes":[]}],"attributes":[]}],"advisements":[],"position":0,"attributes":[]}],"attributes":[{"position":0,"content":"**REFER TO CATALOG**"},{"position":1,"content":"**REFER TO TOP OF AGREEMENT**"}],"showConjunctionBetweenSections":false,"academicYearId":0,"institutionParentId":0,"instruction":{"type":"Following","id":"5757d2c1-4ef1-4767-c75a-08de0ffd5259","selectionType":"Complete"},"advisements":[],"groupId":"51d76f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":15,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"RequirementTitle","content":"COMPUTER SCIENCE","groupId":"716a7445-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":10,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"IMPORTANT TRANSFER INFORMATION","groupId":"54d76f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"RequirementTitle","content":"SECOND COURSE IN ENGLISH COMPOSITION","groupId":"8e3a7945-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":18,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"<p>For admission to the <strong>Environmental Systems Science, Earth and Climate Science Emphasis, B.S.</strong> major, students must earn an overall transferable GPA of 2.8 or better, and must complete classes articulated with the following UC Merced courses by the end of spring term prior to fall enrollment or by the end of fall term prior to spring enrollment. For Admission purposes all major preparation courses require a \"C\" or better.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>REQUIRED</strong> Major Preparation Courses For Transfer:</p><ul><li>CHEM 002 & 002L</li><li>CHEM 010 & 010L</li><li>MATH 011 or MATH 021</li><li>PHYS 018 & PHYS 018L* or PHYS 008 & PHYS 008L</li></ul><p><br></p><p>* Please scroll below for information about how Algebra/Trigonometry based Physics courses can be accepted for admissions purposes.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>Additional Major Preparation Courses:</strong> Recommended Prior to Transfer:</p><ul><li>Complete one Biological or Environmental Systems Science Course: ESS 001 or ESS 002 or BIO 005 or BIO 012 (equivalent to BIO 001)</li><li>Complete one lower division Environmental Systems Science Course: *ESS 010 or *ESS 015 or *ESS 020 or *ESS 034 or ESS 040 or ESS 043 or ESS 050 or ESS 060 or ESS 065</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">*Indicating courses recommended for the Earth and Climate Science emphasis track.</li><li>Complete one Computer Science Course: CSE 005 or CSE 019 or CSE 022 or DSC 011</li><li>Complete the additional Mathematics Requirement: MATH 012 or MATH 022</li><li>Complete one Probability and Statistics Course: BIO 018 or ECON 010 or PSY 010 or MATH 032</li><li>Complete the additional Physics Requirement: PHYS 019 & PHYS 019L or PHYS 009 & PHYS 009L</li><li>Complete one additional lower-division Natural Sciences or Engineering courses. Relevant courses outside of Natural Sciences or Engineering may be allowed with approval.</li></ul><p><br></p><p><strong>AP Exam Score & Course Exemptions</strong></p><ul><li>An AP Biology score of 4 or 5 exempts BIO 005</li><li>An AP Chemistry score of 5 exempts CHEM 002 and CHEM 010</li><li>An AP Environmental Sciences score of 4 or 5 exempts ESS 001</li><li>An AP Mathematics: Calculus AB score of 4 or 5 exempts MATH 011 OR MATH 021</li><li>An AP Mathematics: Calculus BC score of 3 exempts MATH 11 OR MATH 021</li><li>An AP Mathematics: Calculus BC score of 4 or 5 exempts MATH 011 and MATH 012 OR MATH 021 and MATH 022</li><li>An AP Mathematics: Calculus BC Subscore AB score 3 or higher exempts MATH 011 or MATH 021</li><li>An AP Physics: Physics C: Mechanics score of 5 exempts PHYS 008 Visit <a href=\"https://admissions.ucmerced.edu/transfer/requirements\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">https://admissions.ucmerced.edu/transfer/requirements</a> for more specific UC Merced admissions information.</p><p><br></p><p>Prior to Transferring to UC Merced, please be advised of the following for Junior Transfers:</p><p><br></p><p><strong>1) WRI 001 and WRI 010 are admissions requirements:</strong> WRI 001 is fulfilled by IGETC/Cal-GETC 1A - English Composition.
